organisation.name: Test-Bed Environment – System Administrator Lab
organisation.naics_label: Computer Systems Design and Related Services
critical_business_functions: Ubuntu server hardening and configuration testing
regulatory_frameworks: Internal security policy / ad-hoc research governance

risk_profile.regulatory_notes: No regulated data classes in scope. Internal test data only.
risk_profile.threat_notes: No incidents recorded. Primary risk vectors are misconfiguration, unpatched software, and privilege escalation within the VM. Opportunistic SSH brute-force relevant if VM is exposed.

endpoint.edr_recommendation: Wazuh (open source) or Microsoft Defender for Linux
endpoint.patch_management: Manual – apt update/upgrade on Ubuntu; Windows Update on Windows 11 host

virtualisation.network_mode: NAT (outbound internet for updates) + Host-Only adapter (admin access)
virtualisation.snapshot_policy: Ad-hoc snapshots before configuration changes; exported OVA stored on USB

identity.mfa_recommendation: TOTP via Google Authenticator or similar for SSH access
identity.password_policy: Basic – /etc/security/pwquality.conf on Ubuntu; Windows 11 local policy defaults

network.network_monitoring_recommendation: tcpdump or Zeek for test-bed traffic analysis
network.dns_security: Default ISP DNS; no dedicated DNS security layer
network.network_segmentation_notes: VirtualBox NAT/Host-Only adapters isolate VM. No VLAN segmentation.

cloud.container_notes: Docker available for future test-bed expansion

applications.web_server_notes: Apache2 / Nginx may be installed for specific test scenarios
applications.database_notes: PostgreSQL or MySQL may be used for specific test cases
applications.data_storage_notes: VM disk image stored locally on Windows 11 host filesystem

security_operations.siem_recommendation: Wazuh or ELK Stack for log aggregation
security_operations.threat_intel_notes: MITRE ATT&CK Navigator used informally for threat mapping

sbom_reference.notes: No formal SBOM. Software inventory tracked informally via 'apt list --installed' (Ubuntu) and installed applications list (Windows 11). Recommend CycloneDX format via Syft as future improvement.

critical_assets.name: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS Server VM
critical_assets.notes: Primary research and testing asset. Contains all test configurations and workloads.

critical_assets.name: Oracle VirtualBox Hypervisor (on Windows 11 host)
critical_assets.notes: Compromise of hypervisor or host OS would grant full access to guest VM.

critical_assets.name: Windows 11 Host Machine
critical_assets.notes: Secondary asset. Hosts VirtualBox and VM disk images. Must be hardened to protect the VM.

attack_surface.internet_facing_notes: VirtualBox NAT provides outbound-only internet for package updates. No inbound ports exposed externally.
attack_surface.third_party_notes: Ubuntu apt repositories and Windows Update / Microsoft Store only.

security_controls.zero_trust_notes: No formal Zero Trust architecture. Recommend least-privilege sudoers config and SSH key-only access as initial steps.
security_controls.email_security: N/A – no mail server in test-bed
security_controls.backup_notes: VirtualBox VM snapshots before major changes. OVA exported to USB. No automated schedule.
security_controls.incident_response_notes: No formal IR plan. Recommend a basic runbook: snapshot rollback, network isolation (power off VM), evidence preservation.
security_controls.mttd: Not formally measured. Manual review via journalctl and /var/log/auth.log.
security_controls.mttr: Not formally measured. VM snapshot rollback estimated <15 minutes.

data_classification.classification_recommendation: 3-tier scheme: Public / Internal / Confidential applied to scripts, configs, and documentation.
data_classification.encryption_at_rest_recommendation: Enable LUKS full-disk encryption on Ubuntu VM disk.
data_classification.encryption_in_transit_notes: SSH (OpenSSH) enforces encrypted transport for all remote administrative access.

operational_context.uptime_requirements: Non-production. No formal SLA. Best-effort during working hours.
operational_context.high_risk_periods: Active penetration testing exercises should be flagged to avoid false-positive saturation.

governance.change_management: Ad-hoc. Changes documented in local markdown change log. VM snapshots taken before significant configuration changes.
governance.audit_requirements: None mandated externally. Internal self-audit recommended quarterly, aligned to CIS Benchmark for Ubuntu Linux and Windows 11.

recommended_improvements: Enable LUKS full-disk encryption on Ubuntu VM guest disk
Deploy SSH key-based authentication and disable password login
Configure UFW with explicit deny-all default and allowlist only required ports
Deploy Wazuh agent on Ubuntu VM for HIDS and log aggregation
Implement automated daily VM snapshots with retention policy
Generate and maintain a CycloneDX SBOM via Syft
Document a basic incident response runbook
Run CIS Benchmark hardening script on Ubuntu (ubuntu-cis script or manual CIS L1)
Run Microsoft Baseline Security Analyzer or equivalent on Windows 11 host
Evaluate OpenVAS for regular vulnerability scans of the VM
